Texas Pharmacy Tech License

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$84
Processing time
6 to 8 Weeks
Renewal fee
See board
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
Texas State Board of Pharmacy
Path
Endorsement through Texas State Board of Pharmacy
See the steps Primary board page

Board-checked 2026-03-18. Any field still marked "See board" has not been confirmed yet.

Out-of-State Pharmacy Technicians

There is no active interstate compact route for pharmacy technician licensing in Texas. Plan on endorsement through Texas State Board of Pharmacy.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in Texas.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Texas can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: Texas requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: No active compact route here. Plan on endorsement.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

Texas Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed pharmacy technicians applying to practice in Texas.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the Texas State Board of Pharmacy. The application fee is $81.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    Texas requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Request Official Transcripts

    Sent directly from your educational institution.

  4. Step 4

    Submit Your Exam Score Report

    Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

  5. Step 5

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    Texas requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside Texas can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  6. Step 6

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about Allow a minimum of 6 weeks for review and processing once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your Texas Pharmacy Technician license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
$81
Background check
See board
Total estimated cost
See board

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
Allow a minimum of 6 weeks for review and processing (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years, on or before the last day of the original issue month
Renewal fee
See board

Any fee field still marked "See board" has not been confirmed from the board yet.

Temporary License

Texas offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Source: Primary board website

Texas Pharmacy Technician Licensing FAQ

How much does a Texas pharmacy technician license cost?

The Texas State Board of Pharmacy lists an $81 initial pharmacy technician registration fee in its current fee materials. Fingerprinting is paid separately to the vendor.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Texas have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Texas?

The Texas State Board of Pharmacy says to allow a minimum of six weeks for a pharmacy technician application to be reviewed and processed.

Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?

The Texas State Board of Pharmacy says to allow a minimum of six weeks for a pharmacy technician application to be reviewed and processed.
